canonicalEnums.name: ProfessionalWorkObjectType
canonicalEnums.note: DOC-004/DOC-007 reference this type but do not re-enumerate it. DOC-006 fixture emits only a 10-value subset (display). ARTIFACT is in the union but has no dedicated interface. (PWA/PWU_TYPE/UNDERTAKING added for the RPH-DOC-010 PWA-authoring context.) AUTHORING_CONVERSATION added for the durable event-sourced authoring conversation (governed-stream precursor).

canonicalEnums.name: ActorType
canonicalEnums.note: DOC-006 fixture uses only HUMAN, AGENT.

canonicalEnums.name: CardinalityCode
canonicalEnums.appliesTo: PermittedChildRule.cardinality — per-child composition cardinality in a PWA Work Architecture View
canonicalEnums.note: M1 mandatory-exactly-one; M+ mandatory-one-or-more; C1 conditional-zero-or-one; C+ conditional-zero-or-more. Presentation shorthands with executable min/max semantics; the structured applicability predicate for C* is deferred (free-text note for now).

canonicalEnums.name: IntentStatus
canonicalEnums.note: Fixture DOC-006 carries duplicated lifecycleStatus + intentStatus both = UNDER_DISCOVERY; canonical serialized field is intentStatus.

canonicalEnums.name: WorkLifecycleState
canonicalEnums.note: DOC-007 references type WorkLifecycleState (PwuStateChanged) but does not enumerate; DOC-006 fixture shows only SHAPING, PLANNED.

canonicalEnums.name: AssuranceState
canonicalEnums.appliesTo: ProfessionalWorkUnit.assuranceState (PWU-level rollup — NOT the assessment state machine)
canonicalEnums.note: Distinct from AssuranceAssessment.state (15) and AssuranceViewProjection.aggregateDisposition (9). Do not merge.

canonicalEnums.name: WorkRiskProfile.securitySensitivity
canonicalEnums.note: NONE-based scale (no CRITICAL), differs from consequence/uncertainty/irreversibility. DOC-007 §18 RISK_AT_LEAST applies a 4-level LOW..CRITICAL scale across ALL dimensions incl. SECURITY_SENSITIVITY — see conflicts.

canonicalEnums.name: ObligationStrength
canonicalEnums.note: Obligation uses CONDITIONAL; Constraint uses PREFERRED. Keep separate.

canonicalEnums.name: ConstraintType
canonicalEnums.note: DOC-006 fixture uses only SECURITY, BUSINESS, ARCHITECTURAL, TECHNICAL.

canonicalEnums.name: ConstraintStrength
canonicalEnums.note: Uses PREFERRED (vs Obligation CONDITIONAL).

canonicalEnums.name: AssumptionStatus
canonicalEnums.note: Per sponsor rule the 8-value set incl. UNDER_VERIFICATION wins; DOC-006 fixture shows only DISCLOSED.

canonicalEnums.name: ClaimStatus
canonicalEnums.note: DOC-004 §33 observes claimResults[].recommendedStatus='CONTESTED' consistent with this set.

canonicalEnums.name: IndependenceRequirement
canonicalEnums.note: CONFLICT: DOC-002 §17.1 gives only 5 (NONE, DIFFERENT_INVOCATION, DIFFERENT_AGENT, DIFFERENT_MODEL, HUMAN). DOC-004 8-value superset wins.

canonicalEnums.name: FailureSeverity
canonicalEnums.note: 4-value scale WITHOUT INFORMATIONAL. Distinct from AssuranceSeverity (5). Do not merge.

canonicalEnums.name: AssuranceAssessmentState
canonicalEnums.note: CONFLICT: DOC-007 §19 gives 14 (no WAIVER_EXPIRED). DOC-004 15-value superset wins. 'ANY ACTIVE' is a meta-token, excluded. Distinct from PWU.assuranceState and from terminal disposition sets.

canonicalEnums.name: AssuranceDisposition
canonicalEnums.appliesTo: Terminal assurance disposition meanings (semantic outcome vocabulary)
canonicalEnums.note: Full terminal disposition set INCLUDING WAIVED. WAIVED is reached only via the waiver command/event flow, never via a DispositionRule or validator recommendation — those use the 5-value set below.

canonicalEnums.name: AssuranceDispositionRecommendation
canonicalEnums.note: 5-value set — WAIVED deliberately excluded per DOC-004. Identical set is reused by all three fields; author as one shared enum.

canonicalEnums.name: CriterionResultOutcome
canonicalEnums.note: CONFLICT: DOC-004 §7 states these in lowercase prose ('met','partially met','not met','not applicable','unable to determine'); DOC-004 §33 example serializes uppercase. UPPERCASE serialized form wins. Invariant: 'unable to determine' != 'met'.

canonicalEnums.name: AssuranceSeverity
canonicalEnums.canonicalSource: RPH-DOC-007 §21 / DOC-002 §19 / DOC-004 §7 & §9.1 (5 values; all agree)
canonicalEnums.note: 5-value scale WITH INFORMATIONAL. Distinct from FailureSeverity (4). Shared across observation/criterion/finding severity fields.

canonicalEnums.name: ObservationDisposition
canonicalEnums.note: Distinct enum from AssuranceAssessment.disposition despite shared field name 'disposition'.

canonicalEnums.name: ControlAction
canonicalEnums.note: NORMALIZATION into this canonical set: (a) DOC-002 §18 AssuranceAssessment.recommendedControlAction RESHAPE→RESHAPE_PWU, REPLAN→REPLAN_EXECUTION (other 8 of its 10 already match); (b) DOC-002 §37 ControlAction token WAIVE→REQUEST_WAIVER. DOC-004 adds CLARIFY, GATHER_CONTEXT, CHANGE_VALIDATOR, INVALIDATE_DEPENDENTS, REQUEST_HUMAN_DECISION over DOC-002 §37's 18. See openItems re: whether legacy WAIVE should persist as a distinct token.

canonicalEnums.name: DecisionType
canonicalEnums.note: decisionType keeps its own RESHAPE/REPLAN tokens verbatim (a different type from ControlAction) — do NOT normalize these to RESHAPE_PWU/REPLAN_EXECUTION.

canonicalEnums.name: TraceRelation
canonicalEnums.canonicalSource: RPH-DOC-007 §24 / DOC-002 §25 (17 values; DOC-007 governs TraceRelation)
canonicalEnums.note: CONFLICT: DOC-006 fixture §25 shows 12 different edge labels (DERIVED_FROM, DEFINES, REALIZED_BY, REFINED_BY, ALLOCATED_TO, PRODUCES, SUPPORTS, VERIFIED_BY, APPROVED_BY, PROMOTES, PROPAGATES_TO, BECOMES) — those are display-only ASCII-chain annotations, NOT canonical relation tokens.

canonicalEnums.name: ApplicabilityExpressionOp
canonicalEnums.note: Only doc that defines an operator grammar. See applicabilityGrammar for operand shapes.

canonicalEnums.name: RiskLevel
canonicalEnums.note: DOC-007 applies this 4-level scale across all 5 dimensions; DOC-002 §9.2 caps securitySensitivity/regulatoryExposure at NONE..HIGH. See conflicts.

canonicalEnums.name: CompatibilityMilestone
canonicalEnums.note: CONFLICT: DOC-006 gives two shorter display lists — §27 (7: INTAKE, ARCHITECTURE, ASSUMPTION_SURFACING, VERIFY, HISTORICAL_CHECK, REVIEW, COMMIT) and §32.4 (8: INTAKE, ARCHITECTURE, PROPOSE, VERIFY, REVIEW, EXECUTE, VALIDATE, COMMIT). Both docs stress this label MUST NOT be authoritative state — it is a derived projection.

canonicalEnums.name: AssuranceViewAggregateDisposition
canonicalEnums.note: See AggregateAssuranceDisposition (composition) — the two rollups diverge; reconciliation flagged for sponsor.

canonicalEnums.name: AggregateAssuranceDisposition
canonicalEnums.appliesTo: Aggregate assurance disposition — composition-across-policies outcome (semantic rule)
canonicalEnums.note: Must preserve strictest unresolved disposition; 'must not be reduced to a numerical average.' Lacks ASSESSING/WAIVED/INVALIDATED that DOC-007 §26.2 projection carries.

canonicalEnums.name: ValidatorImplementationType
canonicalEnums.note: ValidatorRegistryEntry.implementationType (§35) is typed as a plain string, not this union.

errorCodes.meaning: Structural (JSON Schema) validation of the payload failed. DOC-007 §25.1 required code; per-code category mapping not given in doc.
Actor lacked authority for the requested action (e.g. approval without sufficient authority, §35.5). DOC-007 §25.1.
Requested lifecycle transition is not permitted. DOC-007 §25.1.
A domain invariant would be violated. DOC-007 §25.1.
Referenced evidence has status INVALIDATED and cannot support an active claim/assessment (§35.4). DOC-007 §25.1.
Validator result failed acceptance rules (§20.2). DOC-007 §25.1.
Assessment/validator references wrong policy version. DOC-007 §25.1.
Result applied to a semantic version other than the one assessed (§35.1). DOC-007 §25.1.
Baseline promotion item version/hash mismatch (§35.3). DOC-007 §25.1.
Duplicate idempotencyKey; no additional domain event produced (§35.7). DOC-007 §25.1.
Outcome of an external operation could not be determined. DOC-007 §25.1.

DOC-004 §34.1 — validator failed to EXECUTE. NOT an assurance rejection: yields an execution error + incomplete assessment + retry/alternate-validator/escalation. Also a state-machine state (ASSESSING→VALIDATOR_FAILED, §30). Only backticked error token in DOC-004.

fields.note: DOC-007 §7 adds this; absent in DOC-002 §4
DOC-002 §4 only; DROPPED in DOC-007 §7 canonical envelope — see conflicts
mandatory for updates to existing aggregates
DOC-007 §8 adds; prevents repeated business effects
DOC-007 §20 field; absent in DOC-004 §4.2 version
DOC-007 §18 op-grammar; DOC-004 §5.1 alternatively uses ApplicabilityRule structured fields — see conflicts
DOC-004 §3.1 has this as REQUIRED; DOC-007 §17 OMITS it — flagged for sponsor
object required; all four inner fields optional
one of the per-policy Finding.* code enums
plain string here, NOT the §4.1 union

applicabilityGrammar.notes: Canonical operator grammar = RPH-DOC-007 §18 ApplicabilityExpression (discriminated union on `op`). Operand shapes per op: ALL{operands:ApplicabilityExpression[]}; ANY{operands:ApplicabilityExpression[]}; NOT{operand:ApplicabilityExpression}; EQUALS{path,value}; IN{path,values[]}; CONTAINS{path,value}; EXISTS{path}; RISK_AT_LEAST{dimension:RiskDimension, level:RiskLevel} where dimension in {CONSEQUENCE,UNCERTAINTY,IRREVERSIBILITY,SECURITY_SENSITIVITY,REGULATORY_EXPOSURE} and level in {LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL}. Expressions are declarative and MUST NOT execute arbitrary code (§18/§39). NO OTHER DOC DEFINES OPERATORS: DOC-002 references type ApplicabilityRule (ConstraintObject.applicability §11.1; AssurancePolicy.applicabilityRule §17.1) but never defines its structure. DOC-004 §5.1 ApplicabilityRule offers structured condition FIELDS (objectTypeConditions, pwuKindConditions, lifecycleTriggers, eventTriggers, riskConditions, semanticChangeConditions, requiredTags, excludedTags, expression) plus an opaque `PolicyExpression` (also used by DispositionRule.condition §10.2, WaiverRule.revalidationTrigger §12.1, EscalationRule.trigger §13) whose operator grammar is undefined. DOC-006 gives only prose applicability rules (§29.4) implying object-type/risk-tier applicability + a change-surface trigger set {public interface|data boundary|security boundary|migration strategy|irreversible commitment}. Recommendation: adopt DOC-007 §18 op set as the single canonical grammar; treat DOC-004 PolicyExpression as an alias for ApplicabilityExpression (sponsor confirmation needed).

conflicts.divergence: DOC-002 has 5 (NONE, DIFFERENT_INVOCATION, DIFFERENT_AGENT, DIFFERENT_MODEL, HUMAN). DOC-004 has 8 (adds DIFFERENT_CONTEXT_INSTANCE, DIFFERENT_PROVIDER, ORGANIZATIONALLY_INDEPENDENT).

conflicts.docs: DOC-002 §37 (18) vs DOC-002 §18 recommendedControlAction (10) vs DOC-004 §11 (23)
conflicts.divergence: DOC-002 §37 uses RESHAPE_PWU/REPLAN_EXECUTION and token WAIVE; DOC-002 §18 uses shorter RESHAPE/REPLAN; DOC-004 §11 adds CLARIFY, GATHER_CONTEXT, CHANGE_VALIDATOR, INVALIDATE_DEPENDENTS, REQUEST_HUMAN_DECISION, REQUEST_WAIVER and has no WAIVE token.
conflicts.resolution: Canonical = DOC-004 §11 23-value superset. Normalize on ingest: §18 RESHAPE→RESHAPE_PWU, REPLAN→REPLAN_EXECUTION; §37 WAIVE→REQUEST_WAIVER. DecisionType RESHAPE/REPLAN are a SEPARATE enum and stay verbatim.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-004 governs ControlAction; take fullest superset + record normalization.

conflicts.topic: Assurance assessment STATE vs terminal DISPOSITION conflation
conflicts.divergence: DOC-002 §18 AssuranceAssessment.disposition = 8 values mixing in-flight PENDING/ASSESSING with terminal outcomes. DOC-007 §19 assessmentState = 14. DOC-004 §30 state machine = 15. DOC-004 §10.1 disposition meanings = 6 (incl WAIVED); §10.2/§4.2/DOC-007 §19.3 = 5 (excl WAIVED).
conflicts.resolution: Separate axes: (a) AssuranceAssessmentState = DOC-004 §30 (15, incl WAIVER_EXPIRED); (b) AssuranceDisposition meanings = DOC-004 §10.1 (6, incl WAIVED, waiver-flow-only); (c) AssuranceDispositionRecommendation = 5 (WAIVED excluded) for validator/rule/completed-event. DOC-002 §18 PENDING/ASSESSING are states, not dispositions.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-004 governs assessment state + disposition; DOC-007 §19.3 confirms the 5-value completed set.

conflicts.divergence: DOC-007 omits WAIVER_EXPIRED; DOC-004 includes it (WAIVED→WAIVER_EXPIRED transition).
Fixture uses DEFINES, REALIZED_BY, REFINED_BY, ALLOCATED_TO, VERIFIED_BY, APPROVED_BY, PROPAGATES_TO, BECOMES — none of which exist in the canonical 17.
conflicts.resolution: Adopt DOC-007 §24 17-value set. Fixture edge labels are display-only, non-authoritative.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 is latest serialized contract for TraceRelation; DOC-006 fixture is lowest authority (display labels only).

conflicts.divergence: DOC-004 prose: 'met','partially met','not met','not applicable','unable to determine'. DOC-007 serialized: MET, PARTIALLY_MET, NOT_MET, NOT_APPLICABLE, UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 latest serialized contract + DOC-007 §4.5 mandates UPPERCASE_SNAKE_CASE enums.

conflicts.divergence: DOC-002 has authorityId? and no schemaVersion. DOC-007 adds schemaVersion (required) and drops authorityId.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 is latest serialized contract for envelopes.

conflicts.divergence: DOC-004 §3.1 has riskProfiles:AssuranceProfileRule[] (REQUIRED) and makes optionalEvidence/waiverRules optional. DOC-007 §17 omits riskProfiles and makes optionalEvidence/waiverRules required.
conflicts.resolution: Envelope shape follows DOC-007 §17; retain riskProfiles as an optional field pending sponsor decision (its omission from the serialized contract looks unintended).
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 governs serialized envelopes; DOC-004 governs assurance semantics — genuine tension, flagged.

conflicts.divergence: DOC-007 projection adds ASSESSING, WAIVED, INVALIDATED; DOC-004 composition = {REJECTED, EVIDENCE_REQUIRED, UNASSESSED, INCONCLUSIVE, CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED, SATISFIED}.
conflicts.resolution: Keep as two distinct enums: AssuranceViewAggregateDisposition (read-model, DOC-007 §26.2) vs AggregateAssuranceDisposition (composition rule, DOC-004 §28.2). Flag for reconciliation.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 governs the serialized projection field; DOC-004 governs the semantic composition; not a same-field conflict.

conflicts.resolution: Adopt DOC-007 §26.3 currentMilestone (11, superset). Both DOC-006 lists are display-only; docs explicitly forbid using phase labels as authoritative state.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 latest serialized projection; DOC-006 fixture lowest authority.

conflicts.divergence: DOC-007 adds commandSchemaVersion, targetAggregateType, idempotencyKey (command); eventSchemaVersion (event); providerId (actor).
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 latest serialized contract for envelopes.

conflicts.docs: DOC-002 §4.2 (pwu_, 4 prefixes) vs DOC-007 §5.1 (18 prefixes, <prefix>_<ULID>) vs DOC-006 fixture (trailing-underscore, +dcp_/rcp_/obl_/art_/evreq_/ver_/amb_/human_/agent_/exec_)
conflicts.divergence: DOC-002 shows prefixes with trailing underscore; DOC-007 defines the underscore as separator (prefix token has none). Fixture adds prefixes for object types DOC-007 does not register.
conflicts.resolution: Canonical registry = DOC-007 §5.1 tokens (no trailing underscore). obl/art/dcp/rcp carried from the fixture as UNRATIFIED candidates for the 4 uncovered union types; extra fixture prefixes (evreq/ver/amb/human/agent/exec) are for non-union sub-objects/actors and excluded pending sponsor.
conflicts.authorityRule: DOC-007 is latest serialized contract for ids; DOC-006 fixture ids are display labels only.

conflicts.divergence: Fixture emits only 10 objectType literals; ExecutionPlan/AssurancePolicy have no literal shown in any fixture JSON.
conflicts.authorityRule: Domain model doc over fixture convenience.

conflicts.divergence: AssurancePolicy.failureSeverity omits INFORMATIONAL; observation/criterion/finding severity includes it.
conflicts.resolution: Keep as two separate enums (FailureSeverity 4, AssuranceSeverity 5). Do not merge.
conflicts.authorityRule: Both authoritative for their own fields; distinct member sets.

openItemsForSponsor.why: Only fixture-observed values exist (DOC-006: PRODUCT_REALIZATION, INTENT_AND_PRODUCT_DEFINITION, ARCHITECTURE_DEFINITION); DOC-006 §9 hierarchy names ~9 more branches with no literal. No schema doc enumerates pwuKind. AssurancePolicyDefinition.applicability keys on pwuKindConditions, so a closed set is needed for Zod. Sponsor must supply the authoritative enum.
Only 3 fixture-observed values (PRODUCT_INTENT, PRODUCT_BOUNDARY, CONSTRAINT_CATALOG); no schema doc defines the enum. Almost certainly non-exhaustive.

Fixture-only, domain-instance-specific strings (TENANT_IDENTITY_MISMATCH/OFFLINE_AUDIT_CONFLICT; REJECT_RECOMPOSITION/RESHAPE_MOBILE_ARCHITECTURE). These read as data, not a closed system enum — should likely be free-form strings unless sponsor defines a registry.
DOC-002 §37 has WAIVE; DOC-004 §11 (canonical) has REQUEST_WAIVER instead. We normalize WAIVE→REQUEST_WAIVER. Confirm WAIVE need not persist as a distinct action.
REQUIRED in DOC-004 §3.1 but entirely absent from the DOC-007 §17 serialized contract. Confirm whether the serialized contract intentionally drops risk-profile-driven policy rules.

DOC-007 §25.1 lists 15 required error codes and separately defines the 10-value RphError.category enum, but gives NO code→category mapping. Left blank rather than guessed. Sponsor must fix the mapping (and whether DOC-004 VALIDATOR_FAILED becomes an RphError code or stays a state).

DOC-007 §26.2 (9) and DOC-004 §28.2 (6) describe the same rollup concept with different value sets. Confirm whether the projection field should equal the composition set or intentionally add ASSESSING/WAIVED/INVALIDATED.

These 4 union object types have no DOC-007 §5.1 prefix; only fixture forms (obl_/art_/dcp_/rcp_) exist. Ratify the canonical prefix tokens. Also decide prefixes for non-union sub-objects (EvidenceRequirement, VerificationCriterion, Ambiguity, actors) seen only in the fixture.

DOC-004 uses an opaque PolicyExpression (DispositionRule.condition, WaiverRule.revalidationTrigger, EscalationRule.trigger, ApplicabilityRule.expression) with no defined operators. Confirm it may be unified with the DOC-007 §18 ApplicabilityExpression op set.
DOC-006 fixture carries both lifecycleStatus and intentStatus (both UNDER_DISCOVERY). Confirm intentStatus is the single canonical field and lifecycleStatus is the generic ObjectEnvelope mirror.

Fixture recommends a lowercase mixed set (satisfied/conditionally satisfied/rejected/inconclusive/residual uncertainty/severity/evidence sufficiency) mixing dispositions with dimensions. Not a field enum; superseded by DOC-004 disposition sets. Noted so it is not mistaken for a schema enum.
